design_philosophy: Retro 80s TASCAM Portastudio meets Cyberpunk 2077. Highly tactile, skeuomorphic-leaning 'hardware' elements (sliders, chunky buttons) combined with neon cyberpunk glow and a strict 8-track limitation.

layout_and_spacing.container: w-full min-h-screen bg-background_base text-text_primary p-4 md:p-8 font-body

component_path.top_bar_lcd: A faux-LCD panel at the top center of the screen displaying BPM, Metronome Status, and Measure/Beat position. Needs scanlines overlay.
component_path.timeline_view: A large central container spanning the full width, housing 8 horizontal rows for waveform visualization. Must support drag-and-drop file imports.
component_path.mixer_section: Bottom section organized as a 9-column grid (8 channel strips + 1 master/transport strip).
component_path.channel_strip: Vertical column containing EQ knobs (Hi/Mid/Low), Reverb/Delay sends, Pan knob, Mute/Solo buttons, Record Arm button, and a tall analog-style volume slider.
component_path.transport_controls: Chunky, hardware-like buttons (Play, Pause, Stop, Record, Rewind, Fast-Forward) located in the Master section.
component_path.analog_slider: Built using standard input[type=range] but heavily styled with CSS to look like a physical tactile slider with a raised thumb and recessed track.

motion_and_visuals.textures: CSS repeating-linear-gradient for scanlines on the LCD screen. Heavy use of dark borders and inset shadows on panels to simulate physical casing.

image_urls.description: Close-up shot of a professional audio mixer with slide controls in warm lighting
Close-up of vintage audio equipment sliders

instructions_to_main_agent: Strictly enforce the 8-track limit in the state management. Render exactly 8 tracks in both the timeline and the mixer.
Use IndexedDB (e.g., via localforage) to store project metadata and audio data, as browser localStorage will easily exceed the 5MB limit for audio files.
Implement the Web Audio API for all audio processing. Use BiquadFilterNode for Hi/Mid/Low EQ, DelayNode for Analog Delay, and ConvolverNode for Hall Reverb.
Create the 'Bounce' feature: Allow users to select multiple tracks and merge them into a single track, freeing up the original slots.
Implement the Built-in Metronome using a precise Web Audio API scheduling loop (not setInterval) tied to the project's BPM.
Implement Measure/Beat-based Punch In/Out: Add UI inputs for start and end measures, and automatically trigger the recording state when playback hits the start measure.
Render waveforms using the Canvas API by extracting audio buffer data. Use the accent_cyan (#05D9E8) or accent_pink (#FF2A6D) colors for the waveform stroke.
Ensure the analog volume sliders look tactile using Tailwind shadows (e.g., inset tracks, raised thumbs). Do NOT use default browser slider styles.
All interactive and key informational elements MUST include a 'data-testid' attribute (e.g., data-testid='track-1-volume-slider', data-testid='play-button', data-testid='lcd-bpm-display').
Install and use @phosphor-icons/react for the dense dashboard iconography.
